evals.name: build-expense-manager
evals.description: Build a new Retool app from a description — tests NEW workflow with crud + master-detail patterns
evals.prompt: Build me a Retool app for managing employee expenses. I need a table showing all expense reports with columns: employee name, amount, category, status, and date. Users should be able to create new expenses via a modal form, edit existing ones in a side panel, and approve/reject by changing status. Output a zip I can import into Retool.
evals.expected_output: A complete Retool app with CRUD operations, modal form, side panel editing, and status management
expectations: A main.rsx file exists in the app directory
A functions.rsx file exists with SQL queries
A Table component exists with columns for employee_name, amount, category, status, and date
A ModalFrame with Form for creating expenses exists
A SplitPaneFrame detail pane for editing exists
INSERT query with form binding exists
UPDATE_BY query with filterBy and success events exists
DELETE_BY query with requireConfirmation exists
Event chains: mutate queries trigger refresh of SELECT query on success
All mutation queries have runWhenModelUpdates={false}
validate_app.py passes with 0 FAIL
A zip file is produced that can be imported into Retool

evals.name: add-search-filter
evals.description: Add features to an existing CRUD Table App — tests EDIT workflow with client-side filtering
evals.prompt: I have a CRUD Table App. Add a search bar and a status filter dropdown above the table. Use client-side setFilterStack filtering instead of SQL WHERE clauses.
evals.expected_output: Modified CRUD Table App with search bar and status filter using client-side filtering
expectations: A TextInput component exists for search functionality
A Select component exists for status filtering
A JavascriptQuery exists that builds filter stack from widget values
A lib/applyFilters.js file exists
searchInput and statusFilter are positioned on the same row above the table
All position entries have col + width <= 12
validate_app.py passes with 0 FAIL
A zip file is produced

evals.name: improve-master-detail
evals.description: Improve an existing Master-Detail App — tests IMPROVE workflow with production patterns
evals.prompt: Review my Retool app and make it production-ready with best practices.
evals.expected_output: Improved Master-Detail App with production-ready patterns
expectations: Destructive queries have requireConfirmation={true}
Forms have loading and disableSubmit states
Row re-selection after update via selectRow event
Selection cleared after delete if delete exists
validate_app.py passes with 0 FAIL
A zip file is produced

evals.name: basic-customer-lookup-table
evals.description: Simple customer lookup with table, detail panel, and search — tests foundational table+detail+search pattern
evals.prompt: Build me a Retool app called 'Customer Lookup' that connects to our PostgreSQL database resource called 'production_pg'. It should have a table showing customers with columns: customer_id, full_name, email, phone, signup_date, and status (which can be 'active', 'inactive', or 'churned'). When I click a row, a panel on the right side should show the customer details in a read-only key-value format. Add a text input at the top to search customers by name or email. The table should load automatically when the page opens.
evals.expected_output: A Retool app with a customer table, detail panel, and search input connected to PostgreSQL
expectations: A main.rsx file exists in the app directory
A functions.rsx file exists containing at least one SqlQuery element
The SqlQuery uses resourceName or resourceDisplayName containing 'production_pg'
The SqlQuery has a run-on-page-load configuration (no runWhenModelUpdates={false} on SELECT, or explicit trigger)
The SQL query selects customer_id, full_name, email, phone, signup_date, and status columns
A Table component exists with Column children for each of the 6 specified columns
A TextInput component exists for search functionality
The SQL query includes a WHERE clause that filters by name or email using the search input value
A KeyValue or Container component exists to display selected row details
The detail panel references the table's selected row (e.g., table.selectedRow)
A metadata.json file exists in the app directory

evals.name: order-management-crud-form
evals.description: Full CRUD with modal form, delete confirmation, and colored status tags — tests complete CRUD lifecycle
evals.prompt:
Create a Retool app called 'Order Management' connected to a MySQL resource named 'orders_db'. I need:
1. A table of orders showing: order_id, customer_name, order_date, total_amount, and status (pending/shipped/delivered/cancelled)
2. A button labeled 'New Order' above the table that opens a modal with a form to create a new order. The form should have inputs for customer_name (text), order_date (date picker), total_amount (currency input), and status (select dropdown with the four status options)
3. When I submit the form, it should INSERT into the 'orders' table and then refresh the orders table
4. Each row in the table should have an action column with a 'Delete' button that runs a DELETE query with a confirmation dialog saying 'Are you sure you want to delete this order?'
5. The status column in the table should show colored tags: green for delivered, blue for shipped, yellow for pending, red for cancelled
evals.expected_output: A Retool app with full CRUD for orders including modal form, delete with confirmation, and colored status tags
expectations: A Table component exists with columns for order_id, customer_name, order_date, total_amount, and status
A Button component with text 'New Order' or similar exists
A ModalFrame component exists in the app
A Form component exists inside the modal with input components for the four fields
A Select component exists with options for pending, shipped, delivered, and cancelled
A Date or DateTime input component exists for order_date
A SqlQuery for INSERT INTO orders exists in functions.rsx
The INSERT query's onSuccess event triggers the SELECT/list query to refresh the table
A SqlQuery for DELETE exists with requireConfirmation or confirmationMessage attribute
The DELETE query references the selected row's order_id
The SQL queries use resourceName or resourceDisplayName containing 'orders_db'
The status column has tag or color mapping configuration for the four statuses

evals.name: it-asset-inventory-dashboard
evals.description: Dashboard with stats cards, bar chart, REST API, filtering, and CSV export — tests REST queries and chart components
evals.prompt:
Build an IT Asset Inventory app with a dashboard section and an asset list:

**Dashboard section at the top:**
- Show 4 statistic cards: Total Assets, Assets In Use, Assets In Storage, Assets Needing Repair
- Below that, a bar chart showing asset count grouped by category (laptops, monitors, phones, accessories)
- The data comes from a REST API at endpoint GET /api/assets (no pre-configured resource — use direct URL)

**Asset List below:**
- A table with all assets: asset_id, asset_name, category, assigned_to, purchase_date, purchase_price, condition (excellent/good/fair/poor)
- A select dropdown to filter by category
- A select dropdown to filter by condition
- An 'Export CSV' button that triggers the table's CSV download

Output a zip I can import into Retool.
evals.expected_output: A Retool app with dashboard stats, bar chart, REST API data, filtered table, and CSV export
expectations: At least 4 Statistic or Text components exist for dashboard summary cards
A PlotlyChart or chart component exists for the bar chart
A RESTQuery exists in functions.rsx targeting GET /api/assets
A Table component exists with columns for asset_id, asset_name, category, assigned_to, purchase_date, purchase_price, and condition
At least two Select components exist for filtering by category and condition
A Button component exists labeled 'Export CSV' or similar
The REST query runs on page load
Filter components have change events that trigger filtering logic
A .positions.json file exists with entries for all visible components
validate_app.py passes with 0 FAIL
A zip file is produced that can be imported into Retool

evals.name: support-ticket-triage-workflow
evals.description: Multi-query workflow with split panel, dynamic dropdowns, state tracking — tests complex query chaining and state management
evals.prompt:
Build a Support Ticket Triage app connected to a PostgreSQL resource called 'support_db'. The app should help our support team review and assign incoming tickets.

Layout:
- Left side (60% width): A table of unassigned tickets from the 'tickets' table showing: ticket_id, subject, priority (P1/P2/P3/P4), created_at, customer_email, description. The table should sort by priority ascending then created_at ascending by default. Only show tickets where assigned_to IS NULL.
- Right side (40% width): A detail panel that shows when a ticket is selected. It should display the full description in a text area (read-only), the customer email, and have:
  - A select dropdown labeled 'Assign To' populated by a query that fetches agent names from a 'support_agents' table
  - A select dropdown for 'Priority' pre-filled with the selected ticket's current priority
  - A text area for 'Internal Notes'
  - A button 'Assign & Update' that updates the ticket's assigned_to and priority, inserts a row into 'ticket_notes' table with the note text, and then refreshes the tickets table

Add a temporary state variable to track how many tickets have been triaged in the current session (increment by 1 each time 'Assign & Update' is clicked). Show this count at the top of the page as 'Tickets triaged this session: X'.
evals.expected_output: A Retool app with split-panel ticket triage, multi-query chaining, dynamic dropdowns, and session state tracking
expectations: A SqlQuery exists that selects from 'tickets' where assigned_to IS NULL with ORDER BY priority, created_at
A SqlQuery exists that selects from 'support_agents' to populate the agent dropdown
A SqlQuery exists for UPDATE on the tickets table setting assigned_to and priority
A SqlQuery exists for INSERT INTO ticket_notes
All SQL queries use resourceName or resourceDisplayName containing 'support_db'
A Table component exists with columns for ticket_id, subject, priority, created_at, customer_email, and description
A Select component exists for agent assignment that references the support_agents query data
A State element exists in functions.rsx for tracking triage count
A Text or Statistic component displays the triage session count
The Assign & Update button triggers both the UPDATE and INSERT queries
A TextArea component exists for internal notes input

evals.name: content-moderation-queue-advanced
evals.description: Tabbed container with per-tab REST data, PUT actions, JS validation, image rendering — tests upper-bound complexity
evals.prompt:
Build a Content Moderation Queue app. We have a REST API (no pre-configured resource, use direct URLs).

The app should have a tabbed container with three tabs: 'Pending Review', 'Approved', 'Rejected'.

Each tab shows a table of content items fetched from different endpoints:
- Pending: GET /api/content?status=pending
- Approved: GET /api/content?status=approved
- Rejected: GET /api/content?status=rejected

Columns in all tables: content_id, title, author, submitted_at, content_type (image/text/video), thumbnail_url

The thumbnail_url column should render as an image in the table (not raw text).

Below the Pending tab's table, add a review section that appears only when a row is selected:
- Show the content title as a header
- If content_type is 'image', show the full image using an Image component
- Two buttons side by side: 'Approve' (green) and 'Reject' (red)
- A text area for 'Moderation Notes' (required before rejecting)
- Approve calls PUT /api/content/{content_id} with body {"status": "approved"}
- Reject calls PUT /api/content/{content_id} with body {"status": "rejected", "notes": "<notes text>"}
- After either action, refresh all three tables
- Add a JavaScript query that validates the notes field is not empty when rejecting, and shows a notification if empty

At the top of the app, show three statistic cards with counts: 'Pending', 'Approved', 'Rejected' — derived from the lengths of the three query results.
evals.expected_output: A Retool app with tabbed content moderation, REST PUT actions, JS validation, and image rendering
expectations: A Container component exists with three Views/Tabs for Pending, Approved, and Rejected
Three RESTQuery elements exist, each targeting /api/content with different status parameters
Table components exist within each tab, each referencing its corresponding query
At least one table column is configured to display thumbnail_url as an image
A RESTQuery for PUT /api/content exists for the approve action
A RESTQuery for PUT /api/content exists for the reject action with body containing status and notes
A JavascriptQuery exists that performs validation on the notes field before rejection
An Image component exists in the review section for displaying content
Three Statistic or Text components exist showing counts derived from query result lengths
Button components exist labeled 'Approve' and 'Reject' with color/style attributes
A TextArea component exists for moderation notes
validate_app.py passes with 0 FAIL
